Class Student

java.lang.Object
logic.entity.Student
public class Student extends Object
The player-controlled character that navigates the tile map.

Each game tick update() is called by the main loop. The student moves one tile in currentDirection (set by key-press events) then resets the direction to Directions.NONE, implementing hold-key movement. A brief invincibility window after taking damage prevents rapid consecutive hits.

  • Field Details

    • MAX_HP

      public static final int MAX_HP
      Maximum hit points for the student.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • Student

      public Student(int x, int y)
      Creates a student at the given tile coordinates and loads the character sprite.
      Parameters:
      x - initial column (0-based)
      y - initial row (0-based)

  • Method Details

    • update

      public void update()
      Advances the student by one game tick: decrements invincibility, moves one tile in the current direction (if walkable), then resets direction to Directions.NONE.

    • setDirection

      public void setDirection(Directions dir)
      Sets the movement direction for the next tick. Also records the direction as lastDirection so spells fire the correct way even when the student is stationary.
      Parameters:
      dir - the desired direction; Directions.NONE or Directions.STAY skips recording

    • shoot

      public void shoot()
      Fires a spell in lastDirection using the selected character's element. Consumes one bullet; does nothing if the student has no bullets remaining.

    • answerQuestion

      public void answerQuestion()
      Opens the question popup if the student is standing on a QuestionMark tile. Does nothing if no matching tile is found.

    • takeDamage

      public boolean takeDamage()
      Applies one point of damage and starts the invincibility window.
      Returns:
      true if damage was applied; false if the student is currently invincible and the hit was ignored

    • isDead

      public boolean isDead()
      Returns true when the student's HP has reached zero.
      Returns:
      true if the student is dead

    • getHp

      public int getHp()
      Returns the current hit points.
      Returns:
      current HP (never below 0)

    • getX

      public int getX()
      Returns the student's current column position on the tile grid.
      Returns:
      tile column (0-based)

    • getY

      public int getY()
      Returns the student's current row position on the tile grid.
      Returns:
      tile row (0-based)

    • getPower

      public int getPower()
      Returns the spell damage multiplier (increases as the student answers questions correctly).
      Returns:
      current power level (minimum 1)

    • addPower

      public void addPower()
      Increments the spell damage multiplier by one.

    • getBulletsCount

      public int getBulletsCount()
      Returns the number of spell shots remaining.
      Returns:
      bullet count

    • setImage

      public void setImage(String img)
      Replaces the student's on-screen sprite with the image at the given path. Creates the ImageView on first call.
      Parameters:
      img - classpath-relative path to the image file

    • getImageView

      public javafx.scene.image.ImageView getImageView()
      Returns the JavaFX node used to render the student on the game board.
      Returns:
      the student's ImageView
